title: QUIC Server-Role CertificateVerify Continuation Verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the narrow server-role local CertificateVerify continuation behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam.
expected_result: The server role emits one local CertificateVerify after the supported ClientHello has been accepted, the local ServerHello has been published, local EncryptedExtensions has been published, local Certificate has been published, Handshake keys are available, and the local signing material validates as compatible with the supported single-leaf input; exposes those bytes through the existing Handshake crypto-output seam with correct offset semantics that place the CertificateVerify immediately after ServerHello || EncryptedExtensions || Certificate; appends the local CertificateVerify bytes at the correct transcript boundary immediately after local Certificate; rejects premature, repeated, malformed, incompatible, or unsupported local CertificateVerify progression deterministically; and still leaves server-role commit and wider handshake support unavailable.
procedure: Run the CRT requirement-home tests that cover the supported server-role CertificateVerify continuation path, outbound local CertificateVerify exposure, Handshake key-availability publication ordering, offset semantics, local signing-material availability gating, transcript append behavior, and repeated or conflicting progression rejection.

Run the prerequisite server-role floor tests that cover the supported ClientHello path, outbound ServerHello exposure, the local EncryptedExtensions continuation, and the local Certificate continuation.

Inspect the bridge driver, transcript-progress owner, key-schedule implementation, and connection-runtime pass-through to confirm the slice stays inside the main library seam, keeps runtime or host wiring unchanged, keeps commit unavailable, and does not add native TLS fallback.
scope: This slice verifies only the permanent server-role continuation from peer ClientHello and local ServerHello through local EncryptedExtensions, local Certificate, and local CertificateVerify, plus Handshake packet-protection material availability. It does not prove server Finished, client-certificate authentication, trust-store policy, hostname validation, certificate-path validation, revocation, 0-RTT, 1-RTT, key update, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.
preconditions: The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.
